title: Pitch Practice ~ Communication Skills That Change Your Life ~
description: Let's have fun learning pitch practice with Gal-chan!

scenes.speech: Move your business with communication skills! Let's learn pitching!
scenes.title: Pitch Practice ~ Communication Skills That Change Your Life ~

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: What is Pitch?
scenes.content: Pitch = Present your business to investors, customers, and partners in a short time. Elevator Pitch is an explanation that can be conveyed in 30 seconds to 2 minutes while riding an elevator!
scenes.highlight: Elevator Pitch: Convey your idea to an investor you meet by chance in an elevator

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Three Elements of a Good Pitch
scenes.content: ① Problem (what the customer is struggling with) ② Solution (how your product/service solves it) ③ Why your team is the best - If you can convey these three points in 1-2 minutes, it's a perfect pitch!
scenes.highlight: Golden Pitch Structure: Problem → Solution → Market Size → Business Model → Team → Request

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Steve Jobs' Presentation Techniques
scenes.content: '1,000 songs in your pocket' - iPod presentation. Instead of numbers (1GB), he moved people's hearts by talking about the experience. Rules: ① Keep it simple ② Tell a story ③ Rehearse thoroughly!
scenes.highlight: Jobs' Presentation: 1 slide 1 message, words that appeal to emotions, thorough rehearsal

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Flow of 'Problem → Solution → Evidence → Action'
scenes.content: PAPA Structure: ① Problem (problem statement) ② Agitation (worsening the problem) ③ Solution (presenting a solution) ④ Action (prompting action) - If you present in this flow, the audience will feel 'I want to buy!' or 'I want to support!'
scenes.highlight: PAPA Structure: Common persuasive structure in sales writing, pitching, and presenting worldwide

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Convey with Both Data and Emotions
scenes.content: People won't move with logic (numbers, facts, data) alone. Nor will they trust with emotions (story, empathy, surprise) alone. Combine 'Aristotle's persuasive techniques: Logos (logic), Pathos (emotion), Ethos (trust)'!
scenes.highlight: Three Elements of Persuasion: Balance of Logos (data) + Pathos (story) + Ethos (track record, trust)

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Dealing with Rejection (No)
scenes.content: It's normal to be rejected by investors! Airbnb was rejected 7 times, WhatsApp was rejected by Facebook - The next 'NO' gets closer to 'YES'. When rejected, ask 'why?' and turn it into data to improve your pitch!
scenes.highlight: Airbnb's Fundraising: After being rejected by 7 investors, accepted by Y Combinator → now a 9 trillion yen company

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Practice Makes Perfect
scenes.content: Pitching is determined by 'practice' not 'talent'! Practice in front of a mirror, with family, friends, and record yourself - repeating practice refines your speaking style, timing, eye contact, and word choice. TED speakers practice an average of 200 hours!
scenes.highlight: TED Speakers: Average 200+ hours of preparation and practice for an 18-minute speech

scenes.speech: Let's learn pitch practice with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Global Kids Pitch Contest
scenes.content: Kids TEDx, High School Business Contest, Youths For Youth for junior and senior high school students - There are stages worldwide for kids to present their business ideas! You can grow just by participating. Challenge yourself!
scenes.highlight: High School Business Contest: Some win millions of yen in startup funding at national competitions

scenes.speech: Master your pitch! Next, let's build a team 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
